JOURNAL (previous week):
# Journal — Investing Agent

## 1. Market view

Selective insider accumulation continues on Swedish Large/Mid Caps — two weeks running, Industrivärden-linked buying has been the dominant signal (Essity, Volvo). Quality names remain 10–30% off highs; cash at 78.8%, deployed only into earned signals. Portfolio is Core-only, four positions, no Aggressive sleeve activity. Constructive and patient — next deployment gated on fundamental confirmation or fresh high-conviction signals, not calendar pressure.

## 2. Holdings

**HM-B.ST** — Core | Consumer Discretionary | 4.8%
- **Thesis:** Karl-Johan Persson bought ~1.2bn SEK across six transactions in eight days into an 11% drawdown — among the strongest insider signals possible on a Swedish Large Cap. Starter position pending fundamental confirmation.
- **Sell if:** Profit warning, gross margin collapse, or confirmed structural market-share loss to Shein/Temu. Not on price weakness alone.
- **Status:** `intact`

**ESSITY-B.ST** — Core | Consumer Staples | 7.8%
- **Thesis:** Exceptional 90-day insider cluster — 512 MSEK, 7 buyers, 0 sellers — led by anchor owner Industrivärden and freshly extended by Martinson (129 MSEK, 26 May). Defensive hygiene/staples compounder at 14.4x P/E with 3.4% yield. Sized up to ~8% this week on strengthened signal.
- **Sell if:** Sustained margin deterioration where input costs structurally exceed pricing power, or a Q2 print that contradicts the insider thesis (revenue decline accelerates, margins compress materially). Not on price weakness alone.
- **Status:** `intact`

**THULE.ST** — Core | Consumer Discretionary | 3.7%
- **Thesis:** Eleven insiders including chair, vice chair, CEO, and CFO bought ~25 MSEK in open market over 90 days (two waves: post-Q4 and post-Q1) with zero sellers — exceptional breadth on a quality mid-cap brand 25% off highs. 4.7% FCF yield means we are paid to wait.
- **Sell if:** Insider turns net seller, operating margin compresses below ~13%, or Q2 report (c. 29 July) reveals genuine earnings deterioration rather than cyclical softness.
- **Status:** `intact`

**VOLV-B.ST** — Core | Industrials | 4.9%
- **Thesis:** Industrivärden (controlling owner) deployed ~290 MSEK in a single day near 52-week highs — highest-information insider signal seen in this universe. Net cash balance sheet (~58 BSEK), ~4% yield covered by 16 SEK FCF/share, 9.8% operating margin despite -9.5% YoY revenue. Controlling owners buying near highs is a strong forward-earnings endorsement.
- **Sell if:** Industrivärden becomes a net seller, profit warning, or Q2 print (c. 24 July) reveals order intake collapse rather than cyclical normalisation. Revenue decline alone is not a sell trigger if margins hold.
- **Status:** `intact`

## 3. Watchlist

**SECU-B.ST** — Eight-person cluster including CEO, ~2.1 MSEK same-day buying after ~10% pullback. Best small/mid signal not yet acted on. Revisit at ~148 SEK or when fundamental data available.

**ASSA-B.ST** — Ten operating insiders (CEO, CFO, 7 divisional heads) bought post-Q1 on 2026-04-29; offset by Latour's 2.5 BSEK trim. Entry interest moved down to ~315 SEK (near 200-day MA). Reassess after Q2 print c. 2026-07-28.

**LIFCO-B.ST** — Four-buyer insider cluster (~13 MSEK, post-Q1) on a quality serial acquirer still above the 270 SEK entry target. P/E 36.5 and FCF yield 0.8% demand patience. Watch Q2 print c. 2026-07-24 for margin resilience confirmation.

**ADDT-B.ST** — Three-buyer insider cluster (board + managers) on a quality industrial serial acquirer, but P/E ~41 and FCF yield 1.4% leave no margin of safety at current price. Buy interest ~285–290 SEK (roughly -13% from here). Next earnings ~10 months out; no near-term catalyst.

**BURE.ST** — 19.4 MSEK board-member buy after a +35% run is behaviourally interesting, but cannot underwrite thesis without a current NAV/discount figure. Actionable only after NAV disclosure confirms discount opportunity.

**BILL.ST** — Qviberg's ~13.4 MSEK personal purchase is the real contrarian signal (strip same-day buy/sells). Still a falling knife; revisit on price stabilisation above ~58–60 SEK post-Q2 or when leverage data confirms balance sheet durability.

## 4. Lessons learned / open questions

- **Industrivärden as signal amplifier:** When Industrivärden participates in insider buying (Essity, Volvo), it is categorically higher-information than a single management buy — they are strategic, long-duration, non-tax-driven holders with full board visibility. Weight their participation distinctly.
- **Starter sizing + add discipline is working:** Essity demonstrates the model — small starter on signal, analyst re-confirms thesis, add on strengthened conviction. Volvo is now in the same pattern; the Jul 24 earnings print is the next add/hold/trim decision point.
- **Screener bug — net insider flow (unresolved):** Screener still summing gross Förvärv while ignoring same-day Avyttring. Confirmed false positives on SOBI, AAK, BILL. Fix before acting on any new cluster signal without manual cross-check.
- **Open question:** Both Volvo and Essity report c. 23–24 July. That is a concentrated event-risk window for the two largest positions. Consider whether the portfolio should be deliberately lighter into those prints or whether current sizing (5% + 8%) is already appropriately conservative.

ANALYST NOTES THIS WEEK:
--- Analyst note on ATRLJ-B.ST (Atrium Ljungberg) ---
# Atrium Ljungberg (ATRLJ-B.ST) — Initial Note

**Thesis.** A controlling-family vehicle (Tagehus) just deployed ~30 MSEK into a sharp drawdown caused by a large Finnish pension fund (Varma) liquidating its stake at a 7% discount — i.e. the selling pressure looks technical/forced rather than fundamental. Simultaneously the company landed Ericsson as a 58,000 sqm anchor tenant in Hagastaden, which is materially positive for long-term cash flows. The setup is a quality real-estate name being mechanically sold while informed insiders are buying.

**Positives**
- Tagehus (29.6 MSEK) + Gryningskust (3.0 MSEK) buying into the drawdown is a meaningful net-buy signal from holders who would know the asset best.
- Ericsson HQ lease (58,000 sqm) is a flagship anchor deal — locks in long-term rental income at a major Hagastaden development.
- Drawdown (-26% from 52w high, -17% vs 200MA) has an identifiable, non-fundamental cause (Varma block exit), which is usually a better setup than an unexplained slide.
- Operating margin 37% and gross 60.5% are consistent with a quality property portfolio.

**Concerns**
- Net debt 28.3 BSEK at 101% of equity is high — typical for real estate but leaves the equity sensitive to cap-rate moves and rate expectations, which I cannot assess from this data.
- FCF -1,226 MSEK and FCF yield -6.9% reflect heavy development capex (Hagastaden); whether this is value-accretive or balance-sheet-stretching, I cannot evaluate without the development pipeline detail.
- ROE 3.7% and EPS 1.54 are weak; P/E 17.3 on depressed earnings is not obviously cheap on earnings — the case rests on NAV/property value, which I don't have.
- More sellers may follow Varma; the news flow ("major shareholder wants to sell entire stake") suggests overhang risk is not fully cleared.

**Insider read.** Strong positive. Tagehus is a long-term controlling family vehicle, not a trader — a 29.6 MSEK purchase during a forced-seller-driven drawdown is the kind of signal that historically matters. Reinforced by a board-level buy at 3 MSEK the day before.

**Price read.** 26.75 SEK is -25.8% from 52w high and -16.6% below 200MA, with 30d vol elevated at 35%. The proximate cause (Varma block at 7% discount) is identifiable. Q2 earnings ~33 days out is a near-term catalyst.

**Verdict.** `INTERESTING — fits Core sleeve`

The insider signal + identifiable forced-seller pressure + Ericsson anchor lease is a clean setup. The high leverage and my inability to evaluate NAV/cap-rate sensitivity keeps conviction moderate, not high. PM should consider whether to wait for the overhang to clear or to start a partial position alongside the insider buy.

```json
  "ticker": "ATRLJ-B.ST",
  "name": "Atrium Ljungberg",
  "verdict": "INTERESTING — fits Core sleeve",
  "sleeve_fit": "core",
  "conviction": 3,
  "key_positive": "Controlling family (Tagehus) bought ~30 MSEK into a drawdown caused by a Finnish pension fund's forced block sale, while the company simultaneously landed Ericsson as a 58,000 sqm Hagastaden anchor tenant.",
  "key_concern": "Net debt at 101% of equity and deeply negative FCF from development capex leave the equity sensitive to rates and cap-rates, which I cannot evaluate from price/insider data alone.",
  "would_buy_at": 27.0,
  "would_sell_at": null,
  "notes_for_pm": "Overhang risk: news flow suggests more block-sale supply may follow Varma — consider scaling in rather than full size. Q2 earnings ~2026-07-10 is a near-term catalyst. I cannot assess NAV or cap-rate sensitivity from current data; treat conviction accordingly."
}
```

--- Analyst note on NORION.ST (Norion Bank) ---
**Thesis**

Norion Bank is a small Swedish niche bank trading at P/E ~8.5 with a credible insider/anchor signal: chairman Erik Selin bought ~30 MSEK personally over two days, alongside a board member and management buying. The setup combines a cheap multiple, recent regulatory overhang (90 MSEK FSA fine, now resolved), and a freshly announced 500 MSEK buyback — classic "bad news cleared, capital return + insiders buying" pattern.

**Positives**
- Erik Selin (chairman, well-known Swedish real estate investor — Balder) buying ~30 MSEK of his own money is a high-information signal, not a token gesture.
- 500 MSEK buyback announced 2026-06-05 signals management views shares as undervalued and balance sheet as strong.
- Cluster of insider buys (chairman + board member + management) over a 2-week window — broad-based, not a single actor.
- Valuation looks undemanding: P/E 8.5, ROE 12.8%, decent earnings (EPS 6.91).
- FSA sanction resolved (bank chose not to appeal), removing a known overhang.

**Concerns**
- I cannot evaluate credit quality, loan book mix, or loss provisions from the data I have — for a bank, this is the single most important thing, and the FSA action suggests prior compliance issues.
- Reported FCF of -887 MSEK is shown, but FCF is a misleading metric for banks (loan growth shows as cash outflow); I cannot judge true cash generation without the report.
- Revenue YoY -1.3% — top line is flat to softening; I don't know if NIM is compressing or volumes are shrinking.
- No dividend, and the 90 MSEK fine signals real regulatory risk that may not be fully behind them (one fine often precedes increased scrutiny).
- Strand merger mentioned in news (M4+) — I don't have details on terms, dilution, or integration risk.

**Insider read**

Strongly positive. Selin putting ~30 MSEK of personal capital in two days, joined by a board member and management, is the kind of cluster buy that historically precedes good returns on Swedish Small/Mid Cap. The single small management sell was paired with a larger buy by the same person — net buyer.

**Price read**

Price 58.80 SEK, -19% from 52w highs, -6.6% vs 200ma but +26% over 12m and +5% on the month — the stock has stopped falling and is starting to base after the FSA news. Not extended, not yet breaking out.

**Verdict:** `INTERESTING — fits Core sleeve`

The insider signal + buyback + cleared regulatory overhang + undemanding multiple is a strong combination. The data gap on credit quality keeps conviction at 3 rather than 4 — PM should size accordingly and ideally revisit after the 2026-07-23 earnings.

```json
{
  "ticker": "NORION.ST",
  "name": "Norion Bank",
  "verdict": "INTERESTING — fits Core sleeve",
  "sleeve_fit": "core",
  "conviction": 3,
  "key_positive": "Chairman Erik Selin's ~30 MSEK personal buy plus broader insider cluster, alongside a 500 MSEK buyback at P/E 8.5, just after the FSA overhang resolved.",
  "key_concern": "I cannot assess loan book credit quality from available data, and the recent FSA fine signals real regulatory/compliance risk that may not be fully behind them.",
  "would_buy_at": 60.0,
  "would_sell_at": 78.0,
  "notes_for_pm": "Earnings ~2026-07-23 (~46 days away) — consider whether to take a starter position now on the insider signal or wait for the report to de-risk the credit-quality unknown. Selin's buy averaged ~59-60 SEK, so current price 58.80 is below his entry. If sizing, treat as a Core position but on the smaller end given the data gaps; this is more 'follow a credible insider' than a fully analyzed bank thesis."
}
```

--- Analyst note on SCST.ST (Scandi Standard) ---
# Scandi Standard (SCST.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** A Nordic chicken producer showing accelerating insider conviction — particularly from Grupo Lusiaves (Portuguese poultry group, vice chairman seat), which has been buying repeatedly and in size over six weeks. Combined with positive top-line growth (+8.7% YoY) and strong 12m price performance (+59%), this looks like a quality consumer staples name where informed buyers are signaling something the market may not yet fully price.

**Positives**
- Sustained, broad-based insider buying: 8 distinct buyers, ~30M SEK net over 90 days, zero sellers — this is one of the cleanest insider clusters I've seen.
- The Grupo Lusiaves buying is especially notable: a strategic industry insider (a poultry peer) repeatedly adding through its board seat suggests fundamental conviction, not portfolio rebalancing.
- Revenue growth +8.7% YoY with 3y CAGR +5.2% indicates the business is compounding, not stagnating.
- Price is +24.8% above 200MA but still -12.4% from 52w high — momentum is positive without being euphoric.

**Concerns**
- Operating margin of 4.5% and net margin 2.8% are thin — this is a commodity-adjacent food business with limited pricing power; small input cost shifts (feed, energy) can swing earnings meaningfully.
- Net debt / equity of 71.5% is meaningful; FCF of 142 MSEK against 14.5 BSEK revenue is a 1% FCF margin, leaving little cushion.
- P/E 23.5 is not cheap for a low-margin staples business — much of the bull case is already in the price after a +59% 12m run.
- I cannot evaluate the latest quarterly margin trend from price data alone; the next earnings on ~2026-07-28 will be the real test ~7 weeks out.

**Insider read.** Strongly bullish. Multi-buyer cluster with a strategic peer (Grupo Lusiaves) leading the way is the highest-quality form of insider signal — far stronger than scattered management buys. This is the kind of pattern that historically rewards attention.

**Price read.** Healthy uptrend with room before resistance at 52w high (~164). +24.8% above 200MA is stretched but not extreme; a 5–8% pullback would offer a better entry.

**Verdict.** The insider signal is exceptional and warrants a position, but the thin margins, leverage, and valuation argue for patience and sizing discipline. Earnings on ~Jul 28 is a clear catalyst/risk. `INTERESTING — fits Core sleeve`

```json
  "key_concern": "Thin margins (op 4.5%, net 2.8%) and net debt/equity 71.5% leave little cushion if feed/energy costs move against them; P/E 23.5 already prices in continued execution.",

A starter position now with room to add post-earnings would be the disciplined path. Grupo Lusiaves is a Portuguese poultry group; their continued buying is the single most important data point here."
}
```

--- Analyst note on ASSA-B.ST (Assa Abloy) ---
# Assa Abloy (ASSA-B.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** Assa Abloy is a global quality compounder in access solutions with a long M&A-driven growth record, now trading ~17% below its 52-week high after a sharp pullback. A coordinated, sizable management/board buy cluster on 2026-04-29 — including the CEO, CFO and seven other executives putting in real personal money — is a notable insider signal at these levels. Entry is approaching the 315 SEK area flagged previously, but we are not quite there yet.

**Positives**
- Broad management buying cluster on 2026-04-29 (CEO ~30 MSEK, CFO ~6 MSEK, ~9 executives total ~83 MSEK gross) is an unusually clean insider signal — these are out-of-pocket buys, not equity-program grants.
- Quality profile intact on the metrics visible: operating margin 16.2%, FCF 14.7 BSEK (FCF yield ~4%), ROE 14.5% — consistent with the franchise's historical character.
- Continuing the bolt-on M&A playbook (Canadian docking/loading-dock acquisition announced 2026-06-02, ~1 BSEK revenue target) — small relative to 150 BSEK group revenue, but consistent with strategy.
- Price has corrected meaningfully (-9.8% 1m, -12.7% 3m) into a zone where insiders chose to buy, improving entry risk/reward.

**Concerns**
- Revenue R12 -1.8% YoY — organic momentum has stalled; without access to segment detail or volume/price split, I cannot tell whether this is end-market softness or temporary.
- Latour's 2.5 BSEK block sale (2026-05-26) is the proximate cause of the drop and creates an overhang; a long-time anchor shareholder reducing is a real negative signal even if often portfolio-driven.
- Net debt 65 BSEK (net debt/equity ~60%) is elevated for a serial acquirer — limits flexibility if rates stay high or if a larger deal appears.
- P/E 23 on declining revenue is not cheap; the multiple assumes a return to growth I cannot verify from the data I have.
- Q2 print due ~2026-07-28 (~51 days) is a binary event — buying ahead of it without visibility on organic growth trend is taking event risk.

**Insider read.** Strongly positive on the management side: 10 distinct buyers, real personal capital, clustered on the same day post-Q1 — this is the pattern that tends to matter. The Latour sell is large in SEK but structurally different (portfolio rebalancing by a holding company); I weight the management cluster more heavily, consistent with our principle that insider buying is stronger signal than selling. Net: modestly positive.

**Price read.** 326.80 SEK, -7.1% vs 200ma, -17.2% from 52w high — a real correction, not noise. Still ~3.6% above the 315 SEK reference entry; vol at 19.8% suggests that level is reachable within weeks.

**Verdict.** WATCH — not actionable now, keep on watchlist

```json
  "notes_for_pm": "Two practical considerations: (1) the 315 entry target stands — current 326.80 is close but not there; (2) Q2 earnings ~2026-07-28 is within a typical 4-week minimum hold, so initiating now means owning into a binary event without organic-growth visibility. Prefer to either wait for 315 to print or wait until after Q2. Latour overhang may persist short-term and could provide better entry."
}
```

--- Analyst note on THULE.ST (Thule) ---
# Thule (THULE.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** Thule is a quality consumer discretionary brand currently trading through a cyclical soft patch in outdoor/auto-roof demand. The setup is unusually clean: a held position down 28% from highs while ten insiders — including the CEO, CFO, vice chairman, and broad management — bought ~14 MSEK in open-market purchases over the last 90 days, with zero sellers. That insider cluster on a Large Cap name is a strong signal that those closest to the business see the weakness as cyclical, not structural.

**Positives**
- Exceptionally broad insider buying cluster (10 distinct buyers, no sellers), including CEO Ankarberg and CFO Lawton in March, plus vice chairman Blomquist adding ~11.6 MSEK in May near current price levels.
- Fundamentals remain sound: 46% gross margin, 16% operating margin, 14.5% ROE, FCF of ~1.1 BSEK — this is still a high-quality franchise, not a broken business.
- Revenue grew +5.7% YoY in latest R12, suggesting the cycle may already be turning despite weak share price.
- Reasonable valuation at P/E 20 with a 3.9% dividend yield provides downside support.

**Concerns**
- Net debt / equity of 53% is meaningful; not alarming for a cash-generative business, but limits flexibility if the cycle deepens.
- 3-year revenue CAGR of only +0.9% confirms this has been a multi-year soft period — the post-COVID outdoor demand normalization is real and prolonged.
- I cannot see the latest quarterly commentary, order trends, or inventory levels from the data I have — the July 29 report is the real test of the thesis.
- Stock is in a clear downtrend (-9.9% vs 200ma, -27.6% from 52w high), so timing of any addition is unfavorable; falling knife risk remains.

**Insider read.** This is the strongest possible insider signal short of a take-private bid: full management team plus board buying with own money, across multiple months, no offsetting sellers. On a Large Cap this is rare and meaningful — insiders are explicitly saying the current price undervalues the business.

**Price read.** At 211 SEK, the stock is 28% below its 52w high and 10% below the 200ma, trading just below our cost basis of 226.60 (-7% paper loss). 30d vol of 35% is elevated, indicating the market is repricing actively. Next earnings ~52 days away will be the catalyst either way.

**Verdict.** `HELD: thesis intact`

Would consider modest add below 200 SEK on the strength of the insider signal, but do not chase — the trend is still down and we already have a position. If Q2 report shows weakening margins or guides down further, reconsider; the insider cluster is the primary reason to stay patient."
```json
}
```

--- Analyst note on VIT-B.ST (Vitec Software) ---
# Vitec Software (VIT-B.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** Vitec is a Swedish vertical-software serial acquirer with a long track record of compounding revenue through bolt-on M&A. The stock is down ~48% from its 52-week high and ~40% over 12 months, and three insiders — including a board member at >1 MSEK — have bought in the open market in the last 6 weeks. This combination *can* mark a reset in a quality compounder, but a fresh public short position and a negative trailing FCF require explanation before acting.

**Positives**
- Insider activity is unambiguously constructive: 3 distinct buyers, 0 sellers in 90 days, board member putting >1 MSEK of own money in just last week.
- Gross margin 78.5% and operating margin 18.9% are consistent with a healthy vertical SaaS model; 3-year revenue CAGR of ~19% confirms the compounder profile is intact at the top line.
- Drawdown of ~48% from highs on a name historically priced as a premium compounder means the multiple has compressed materially — if earnings power is intact, this is the kind of setup the Aggressive sleeve exists for.
- Price has stabilised: +8.2% over 3 months and only -2.2% over the last month suggests the worst of the de-rating may be behind.

**Concerns**
- **R12 FCF is -510 MSEK (FCF yield -5%)** despite 11.6% net margin. For a serial acquirer this usually means heavy M&A spend or working-capital swings, but I cannot distinguish "investing through the cycle" from "deteriorating cash conversion" without the cash-flow statement and segment disclosures, which I don't have.
- A new **public short-seller (Capital Fund) disclosed on 2026-06-02** — I cannot see their thesis, but a public short on a previously beloved Swedish compounder is a non-trivial signal and at least partly explains the drawdown.
- Net debt / equity of 53% is manageable but not low for a SaaS roll-up, and combined with negative FCF it constrains acquisition firepower if the equity stays depressed.
- ROE has fallen to 8.9% and P/E is still 22.8 — not a value entry; the bull case requires growth and margins to re-accelerate, which I can't verify from price data alone.

**Insider read.** Strongly positive and the cleanest signal in the file. CFO + board member + management all buying in open market with zero sellers, after a major drawdown, is exactly the pattern that historically rewards attention on Swedish quality names.

**Price read.** -47.6% from 52w high, -11.5% below 200dMA, but 3-month momentum has turned slightly positive — looks like a basing pattern rather than a falling knife. Vol30d at 47.7% is elevated; expect chop.

**Verdict.** The insider signal and quality profile justify interest, but the new short position and negative FCF mean I cannot underwrite a confident entry without sight of the Q1 cash-flow detail and a read on the short thesis. Best handled as a watch into the **July 23 earnings report** (~46 days away), which should clarify both cash conversion and management's response to the short.

`WATCH — not actionable now, keep on watchlist`

```json
{
  "ticker": "VIT-B.ST",

  "key_positive": "Three insiders including a board member buying >1 MSEK in open market with zero sellers, on a quality serial-acquirer SaaS compounder down 48% from highs.",
  "key_concern": "Trailing FCF is -510 MSEK and a new public short-seller (Capital Fund) was disclosed on 2026-06-02 — I cannot evaluate either from the data I have, and Q1 cash-flow detail is needed before committing capital.",
  "would_buy_at": 240.0,
  "would_sell_at": null,
  "notes_for_pm": "Earnings ~2026-07-23 (~46 days). Strong preference to wait for that print to verify cash conversion and hear management address the short thesis before initiating. If price drops into the 230–240 area on no fundamental news before then, a small starter Aggressive position (~3% of portfolio) could be justified on insider signal alone — but full position only post-earnings if numbers confirm. Flag to Journal Keeper: track Capital Fund short thesis if it becomes public."
}
```


RISK CAPS (mirrors constitution §4 — enforced in code):
  - Max 15% single holding (of total portfolio)
  - Max 25% any single sector (of total)
  - Max 10% single Aggressive position
  - Max 20% Aggressive sleeve total
  - Min 30% cash buffer of the Core sleeve
  - Max 90% total equity exposure
  - Max ~10 holdings
  - Min 4-week holding period (full exits)
response_text:
## Portfolio context

Cash sits at 79% — deliberately high. Four Core positions, no Aggressive sleeve, two of four holdings (HM-B, Thule) in Consumer Discretionary at ~8.4% sector exposure. Volvo and Essity (the two largest) both report in late July; that event window matters.

## Decisions

The Analyst delivered three INTERESTING names this week (ATRLJ-B, NORION, SCST) plus two WATCH (ASSA-B, VIT-B) and confirmed THULE as held/intact. All three INTERESTING names are conviction 3 — solid setups, not slam-dunks, each with a meaningful data gap that the analyst explicitly flagged.

**ATRLJ-B (Atrium Ljungberg)** — Cleanest setup of the three. Tagehus controlling-family buy into a *forced* technical seller (Varma block), plus Ericsson anchor lease as fundamental confirmation. The "informed insider buying into mechanical selling" pattern is exactly the kind of asymmetric setup I want. Concerns are real (101% net debt/equity, can't assess NAV), so a starter — not a full size. Overhang risk argues against going big now. Also adds Real Estate sector diversification (currently 0%). **Starter ~5% position.**

**NORION (Norion Bank)** — Erik Selin's 30 MSEK personal buy is a high-information signal. But: I can't evaluate a bank's credit quality from outside, and the FSA fine is recent. Analyst explicitly suggests waiting for the July 23 earnings to de-risk. With Essity and Volvo also reporting that week, I do not want a third unknown-quantity earnings event in the same window. **Pass for now, add to watchlist for post-earnings re-look.**

**SCST (Scandi Standard)** — Grupo Lusiaves cluster is strong, but it's stretched +24.8% above 200MA on a +59% 12m run, thin-margin food business at P/E 23.5, into July 28 earnings. Analyst's own buy target is 135 (current ~160s implied). Chasing extension after a 59% run violates "Never chase a missed move." **Pass.**

**THULE** — held, thesis intact, conviction 4 from Analyst. No action. Below cost basis but the insider cluster is the reason we own it; price weakness alone is not a sell. Will revisit at Q2 (July 29).

**ASSA-B, VIT-B** — both correctly WATCH. No action.

## Risk awareness

After ATRLJ buy: equity exposure rises from 21% → ~26%, cash to ~74% — still very conservative. Consumer Discretionary stays at ~8% (well under 25% cap). Real Estate adds at ~5%. No sleeve crowding. Core cash buffer remains far above the 30% minimum. Three earnings prints land in a ~7-day window (July 23–29: Volvo, Essity, Thule) — I'm comfortable with current sizing into that, and the new ATRLJ position reports ~July 10 *before* that cluster, which actually helps.
